subject: physical_education
jamb_high_priority_topics: History and Foundation of Physical Education

topics.title: History and Philosophy of Physical Education

subtopics.title: Meaning and Importance of Physical Education
common_misconceptions: Physical education is just playing games
PE is not an academic subject
subtopics.teaching_note: Physical education = education through physical activity. Develops physical, mental, social, and emotional well-being. Components: physical fitness, motor skills, knowledge, social development, emotional health. Historical figures: Johann Basedow (modern PE founder), Friedrich Jahn (German gymnastics), Per Henrik Ling (Swedish gymnastics). Nigerian PE: organized sports in schools, NIS (National Institute for Sports) in Lagos, NSF (Nigeria Sports Festival), NSF (National Sports Festival). Benefits: reduces obesity, improves academic performance, develops teamwork, builds character.

subtopics.title: Sprints and Middle Distance
common_misconceptions: Sprinting and jogging use the same technique
Arms are not important in sprinting
The baton can be thrown in relay
subtopics.teaching_note: Sprints: 100m, 200m, 400m. Technique: starting blocks (on your marks, set, go), drive phase, acceleration, maintenance, finish. Arm action: pump vigorously, opposite arm to leg. Middle distance: 800m, 1500m. Requires speed and endurance. Relay: 4×100m, 4×400m. Baton exchange: visual (upward sweep) or non-visual (downward sweep). Changeover zone: 20m. Nigerian athletes: Tobi Amusan (100m hurdles world record holder), Blessing Okagbare, Davidson Ezinwa, Enefiok Udo-Obong.
